{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided, antibody-dependent enhancement is specifically mentioned in relation to dengue hemorrhagic fever. Document [19] states that severe dengue fever, characterized by heightened microvascular permeability and shock syndrome, is often associated with infection by a second dengue virus serotype and the patient's immune response. This aligns with the concept of antibody-dependent enhancement, where a pre-existing antibody response to a different serotype of the virus enhances the infectivity of a new serotype. No other diseases in the options are directly linked to antibody-dependent enhancement.", "answer_choice": "D"}
